_comment: Security classification for every API function that mutates state or exposes sensitive data.
SCHEMA:
{ "<api function name>": "normal" | "strict", ... }
- "normal" (default): cookie session or Bearer token sufficient.
- "strict": caller must provide a fresh sudo-mode token (short-lived,
issued by POST /api/auth/sudo-password after re-entering the governance
password). Applies to destructive or irreversible operations.
RULES:
1. Only list entries that need "strict" treatment. Absence = "normal".
2. Function names must match the exported handler name or the route path
written as METHOD_/api/path (e.g. DELETE_/api/agents/[id]).
3. This file is source-only — it is NOT read by the build pipeline. The
loader at lib/security-registry.ts parses it at runtime.
4. Edit this file to add or remove strict entries. No code change needed;
the middleware + route wrapper read the map on every request.
DEFENCE IN DEPTH:
Even a "normal" route still requires the global /api/* middleware auth
gate (cookie or Bearer), plus the per-route authenticateFromRequest()
check, plus (for agent writes) the gate0Auth authorization check inside
the ChangeTitle / ChangePlugin pipelines. "strict" adds a fourth layer:
a sudo-mode token earned by re-entering the password within the last 60s.
R32 DUAL-PATH (lib/sudo-guard.ts::requireSudoToken):
A "strict" route is gated DIFFERENTLY per caller. USER/UI (cookie
session, no agentId) → must present a fresh sudo token (subject- and
op-bound, one-shot). AGENT (Bearer AID/AMP, has agentId) → NEVER sudo;
the gate is the R28 three-check (AID identity → TITLE privilege via
authorize() → portfolio/mandate token), so a strict entry stays correct
for the USER while agents authorize by their AID. DELETE /api/v1/agents/me
is intentionally NOT strict: it is agent-only (AMP self-deregister), and
sudo there would violate R32 — its authority is DeleteAgent's Gate-0.
